lede: A look at October 20 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Carney Chukwuemeka and Michel Vorm, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Liz Truss
events.desc: Liz Truss steps down as British Prime Minister and leader of the Conservative Party amid the country's political crisis, serving for the least time of any British Prime Minister (49 days).

events.title: Space Shuttle Columbia
events.desc: Space Shuttle Columbia launches on STS-73.

events.title: Aeroflot Flight 6502
events.desc: Aeroflot Flight 6502 crashes while landing at Kuibyshev Airport (now Kuromoch International Airport) in Kuibyshev (now present-day Samara, Russia), killing 70 people.

events.title: Lynyrd Skynyrd
events.desc: A plane carrying the rock band Lynyrd Skynyrd crashes in woodland in Mississippi, United States. Six people, including three band members, are killed.

events.title: Watergate scandal
events.desc: Watergate scandal: "Saturday Night Massacre": United States President Richard Nixon fires U.S. Attorney General Elliot Richardson and Deputy Attorney General William Ruckelshaus after they refuse to fire special prosecutor Archibald Cox, who is finally fired by Solicitor General Robert Bork.

events.title: China
events.desc: China launches simultaneous offensives in Ladakh and across the McMahon Line, igniting the Sino-Indian War.

events.title: Red Army
events.desc: World War II: The Soviet Red Army and Yugoslav Partisans liberate Belgrade.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Thousands of civilians in German-occupied Serbia are murdered in the Kragujevac massacre.

events.title: Greek War of Independence
events.desc: Greek War of Independence: In the Battle of Navarino, a combined Turkish and Egyptian fleet is defeated by British, French and Russian naval forces in the last significant battle fought with wooden sailing ships.

events.title: Pragmatic Sanction of 1713
events.desc: France, Prussia, Bavaria and Saxony refuse to honour the Pragmatic Sanction, and the War of the Austrian Succession begins.

births.name: Laurie Daley
births.line: Australian rugby league player and coach

births.name: Hilda Solis
births.line: American academic and politician, 25th United States Secretary of Labor

deaths.text: Barbara Dane, American folk, blues and jazz singer
Edward B. Greene, American banking, mining, and steel company executive

observances.text: Christian feast days: Hedwig (in Canada, moved from Oct. 16)
Christian feast days: Margaret Marie Alacoque (in Canada, moved from Oct. 16)
